sourceMovie.title: Life of Pi
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Life of Pi works as a adventure movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The adventure and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Perfect Storm
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Life of Pi clicked for you, The Perfect Storm (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Where the Wild Things Are
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Life of Pi clicked for you, Where the Wild Things Are (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and drama, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Life of Pi?
faq.answer: Try The Perfect Storm, Where the Wild Things Are, Eight Below—each shares DNA with Life of Pi while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Life of Pi?
faq.answer: Life of Pi (2012) is best described as Adventure, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Life of Pi worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
